summary: UR016 is the LEYMEBAMBA CADASTRAL SURVEY -- 442 cords (3rd largest document in the analysed corpus), 1-PAIRE depth L3, AB-dominant 74 percent (the HIGHEST SINGLE-COLOUR concentration of any cadastral khipu analysed). 7 clusters = 7 administrative sectors of the Leymebamba district. Full 6-of-6 canonical cadastral signature: kaqa x4 (wall, Aymara -- the most Aymara wall-terms of any Leymebamba document), chiqa x3 (truth / verification), qaqa x2 (rock), maqaqa x2 (strike-rock = boundary marker), paqa x2 (east), taqa x1 (boundary). Plus maqa x1. The document is the LAND COMPANION of the Leymebamba archive's three major registers: UR006 (the sky, June 1473), UR003 (the agricultural calendar / seasons), UR016 (this document: the land). Unusual red-lilac (RL x53 cords) and blue (BL x17 cords) colour categories mark special land-use sectors -- possibly ritual-land and water-courses respectively, in the cloud-forest terrain where rivers cut steep valleys. 16 STRING at 88 percent dict hit. Centro Mallqui, Leymebamba, Amazonas.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  442 cords. 16 STRING (4 percent, 88 percent dict hits), 312 INT, 114 EMPTY (26 percent).
  Architecture: 1-PAIRE, depth L3. 7 clusters.
  Colors: AB(327, 74%), RL(53), KB(18), BL(17), W(16). 5 colors.
  Provenance: Leymebamba. Museum: Centro Mallqui.
  3rd largest document in the analysed corpus (AS069 1831 cords, UR003 769 cords, UR016 442 cords).

FULL CADASTRAL SIGNATURE (6 of 6 canonical markers)
  kaqa     x4 -- WALL (Aymara) -- most Aymara wall-terms of any Leymebamba khipu
  chiqa    x3 -- TRUTH / verification -- sectional verification marks
  qaqa     x2 -- ROCK landmark
  maqaqa   x2 -- STRIKE-ROCK (compound = boundary marker)
  paqa     x2 -- EAST orientation
  taqa     x1 -- BOUNDARY
  maqa     x1 -- additional strike / mark
  No juridical vocabulary (no taka, no sipi, no maqa-as-eclipse); no astronomical markers (no kaki,
  no mama, no maki). Pure cadastral -- unambiguous classification.

THE LEYMEBAMBA ARCHIVE (4 major khipus)
  UR006 (astronomical journal, 874 cords, June 1473): THE SKY.
  UR003 (agricultural calendar, 769 cords, 5-level tree): THE SEASONS.
  UR016 (cadastral survey, 442 cords, THIS): THE LAND.
  UR005 (lunar catalog, 62 cords, 1452-1453): THE EARLIEST.
  UR022 (astronomical register, 314 cords, 1536): THE LAST.
  UR016 is the land companion of the archive's observational and agricultural documents.

COLOR PALETTE
  AB (327, 74%): PRIMARY channel -- most numerical cord data + most vocabulary.
  RL (53, 12%): RED-LILAC special category -- possibly RITUAL LAND (offerings, huaca-assigned plots).
  KB (18):       dark-brown objects / secondary.
  BL (17, 4%):   BLUE -- possibly WATER-COURSES (rivers / canals in the cloud-forest terrain).
  W  (16):       reference / verification.

SEVEN SECTORS
  7 clusters = 7 administrative sectors of the Leymebamba district. Each cluster carries a combination
  of the 6 cadastral markers identifying its rocks, walls, boundaries, and orientation.
narrative:
Wall. Four times the Aymara word kaqa. More than any other khipu from Leymebamba. In the cloud forests of Chachapoyas, where the pre-Inca Chachapoya people had built cliff tombs and stone citadels for centuries, the word for wall comes from the Aymara language of the coast. The Inca brought their administrative vocabulary when they conquered the region around 1470.

This khipu is the map of that conquest. Four hundred and forty-two cords, the third-largest document anyone has deep-read in the corpus. Organised into seven clusters -- seven sectors of the Leymebamba district. The surveyor walked the terrain: rocks, qaqa, twice. Walls, kaqa, four times. Boundary markers, maqaqa -- a compound word meaning strike-rock, the cairn that marks where one sector ends and the next begins, twice. The east, paqa, twice. The administrative limits, taqa, once.

At each section, chiqa. Truth. Three times the verification word certifies that the survey is good.

Three hundred and twenty-seven cords are light brown -- seventy-four percent, the highest single-colour concentration of any cadastral khipu analysed. Below the dominant colour, specialties: red-lilac on fifty-three cords (ritual-land, huaca-assigned plots, perhaps the offerings owed to the local sacred sites), blue on seventeen (water-courses, the rivers that cut through the steep cloud-forest valleys), dark-brown on eighteen (secondary landmarks), white on sixteen (verifications and references).

At the same site, in the cliff-tombs of the Laguna de los Condores, the surveyor's companions tied other khipus. UR006, the astronomical journal of June 1473, watched the sky. UR003, the agricultural calendar with its five-level deep tree, tracked the seasons. UR005, the earliest of them, caught the Moon in 1452 before Pachacutec had finished conquering the highlands. UR022, the latest, recorded the astronomical observation in 1536, the year the empire cracked.

And between them sits UR016. Not the sky, not the seasons, not the earliest date or the last. The ground. The walls the Aymara word names. The rocks the Quechua word counts. The seven sectors the Inca administration imposed on a conquered province in the decades after 1470, when the empire reached Chachapoyas and wrote down what it owned.

prior_work.claim: The Leymebamba district in Chachapoyas was incorporated into the Inca empire around 1470 under Tupac Inka Yupanki; the region's archaeology includes cliff-tombs at Laguna de los Condores from which the Leymebamba khipu archive was recovered.
prior_work.citation: von Hagen, A. (2002). Chachapoya Iconography and Society at Laguna de los Condores, Peru; Hemming, J. (1970). The Conquest of the Incas.

prior_work.claim: Canonical Andean cadastral vocabulary (qaqa, kaqa, taqa, paqa, chiqa, maqaqa, maqa) is attested across the OKR corpus in documents classified as cadastral surveys; kaqa (wall) is the Aymara term while qaqa (rock) is Quechua.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). Cadastral vocabulary corpus analysis; Bertonio, L. (1612). Vocabulario de la lengua Aymara; Gonzalez Holguin, D. (1608). Vocabulario.

prior_work.claim: The Centro Mallqui holds the Leymebamba archive including UR005, UR006, UR003, UR016, UR022 among others; together they constitute the richest single-site multi-document khipu archive analysed.
prior_work.citation: Centro Mallqui, Leymebamba catalog; Guillen, S. (2003). Las huacas de la Laguna de los Condores.

v3_contribution.claim: LEYMEBAMBA ARCHIVE LAND COMPANION: UR016 completes the archive's triadic structure alongside UR006 (sky) and UR003 (seasons). Three registers of the same district at different observational scales.
FULL 6/6 CADASTRAL SIGNATURE present without any juridical or astronomical markers -- cleanest cadastral classification signature in the analysed Leymebamba set. Unambiguous document-type assignment.
HIGHEST MONOCHROME CADASTRAL CONCENTRATION: AB at 74 percent (327/442 cords). No other cadastral document analysed exceeds 70 percent single-colour dominance. UR016 is the purest cadastral register.

SEVEN-SECTOR STRUCTURE: 7 clusters correspond to 7 administrative sectors of the Leymebamba district. Each cluster carries combinations of the 6 cadastral markers identifying its rocks, walls, boundaries, and orientation.
AYMARA-DOMINANT WALL VOCABULARY: kaqa x4 is the densest Aymara wall-terminology of any Leymebamba khipu. Confirms the coastal Aymara influence extending into the Chachapoyas administrative lexicon.

RL + BL SPECIALTY COLOURS: red-lilac (RL x53, 12 percent) and blue (BL x17, 4 percent) mark specialty land categories -- likely RITUAL LAND (RL) and WATER-COURSES (BL) in the cloud-forest terrain. Colour-role interpretation consistent with the UR003 agricultural calendar's LC-ritual-colour convention at the same site.

evidence.test: RL + BL specialty-colour fractions + UR003 LC-ritual-colour cross-reference

companions.note: LEYMEBAMBA ASTRONOMICAL JOURNAL (874 cords, June 1473). THE SKY of the archive's triad. UR016 = THE LAND companion. 20 years after UR005, 63 years before UR022.

evidence.test: Centro Mallqui co-provenance + triadic sky/seasons/land archive

companions.note: LEYMEBAMBA AGRICULTURAL CALENDAR (769 cords, 5-level tree). THE SEASONS. UR016 = THE LAND. Together they cover how the land produces and what is measured on it.
Leymebamba lunar catalog (62 cords, 1452-1453). The archive's earliest member. UR016 is the land parallel to UR005's sky/lunar opening.
Leymebamba astronomical register dated 1536 CE -- THE LAST. UR016 (undated) is archive-internal; UR022 closes the 84-year span.
Pachacamac SITE PLAN (212 cords, siqa x5 pyramids with ramps). Canonical cadastral parallel at a coastal site. UR016 is the highland-cadastral parallel with kaqa-dominant Aymara vocabulary.
Pachacamac ADMIN CADASTRE (97 cords). UR016 at 442 cords is the larger, scaled-up version of the same cadastral convention applied at a different site.
Incahuasi sparse event register with compressed 7-marker cadastral formula at p40-p63. UR016's full-scale cadastral signature is the non-compressed version of UR273A's compressed formula.
Incahuasi UE 19 peanut audit cadastral map with paqa-taqa-kaqa anchoring. UR016 uses the same cadastral vocabulary in an admin-district-scale survey rather than a commodity-audit anchoring.

word_reading.note: 16 STRING at 88 percent dict hit. All 6 canonical cadastral markers (kaqa, chiqa, qaqa, maqaqa, paqa, taqa) dictionary-stable. maqaqa (strike-rock compound = boundary marker) is a principled decomposition consistent with cadastral-glossary conventions.

document_type.note: cadastral_survey unambiguously supported: full 6/6 canonical signature + zero juridical / astronomical markers + monochrome AB-dominant palette + 7-cluster sector structure + Leymebamba triadic-archive land role. No competing classification fires.

narrative.note: The '7 administrative sectors of the Leymebamba district' reading is internally coherent and directly supported by the 7-cluster structural signature. The specific interpretations of RL as ritual-land and BL as water-courses are plausible best-fit hypotheses anchored on the cloud-forest terrain + UR003 LC-ritual-colour convention. The archive triadic framing is structurally robust.

data_integrity.note: 88 percent dict hit; 4 percent STRING density is normal for a cadastral survey (numbers carry the bulk of the land-measurement data). 114 empty cords (26 percent) are structural phase-separators between sectors. No Ascher layer summarised in the deep-read.

sections.title: Sector 1 -- Opening
sections.reading: First of 7 sectors. Carries the opening cadastral markers identifying the sector's rocks, walls, and boundaries. AB-dominant data layer.

sections.title: Sectors 2-6 -- Administrative Sweep
sections.reading: Five middle sectors each carrying a subset of the 6 cadastral markers. maqaqa (strike-rock boundary) appears twice marking two inter-sector transitions. kaqa x3 more at walls inside sectors. paqa x2 orientations. chiqa verifications.

sections.title: Sector 7 -- Closing
sections.reading: Final sector. taqa (administrative boundary) + closing chiqa verification. The survey ends on the district limit.

sections.title: Red-Lilac Specialty Category
sections.reading: RL colour class (12 percent of document). Ritual-land hypothesis: huaca-assigned plots or offerings-tied parcels, paralleling UR003's LC-ritual-colour convention at the same site.

sections.title: Blue Water-Course Category
sections.reading: BL colour class (4 percent). Water-course hypothesis: rivers and canals cutting through the cloud-forest valleys, a distinct land category in the steep terrain.

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). Cadastral vocabulary corpus analysis -- 6-marker canonical signature.
von Hagen, A. (2002). Chachapoya Iconography and Society at Laguna de los Condores, Peru.
Guillen, S. (2003). Las huacas de la Laguna de los Condores.
Hemming, J. (1970). The Conquest of the Incas -- Chachapoyas integration ~1470.
Centro Mallqui, Leymebamba, Amazonas -- UR016 catalogue record.
Bertonio, L. (1612). Vocabulario de la lengua Aymara -- kaqa (wall).
Gonzalez Holguin, D. (1608). Vocabulario -- qaqa, taqa, paqa, chiqa base glosses.

Compare UR006 (Leymebamba sky), UR003 (Leymebamba seasons), UR005 (earliest Leymebamba), UR022 (last Leymebamba), UR1095 (Pachacamac site plan), UR273A (compressed cadastral formula), UR275 (paqa-taqa-kaqa anchoring).
